title: auricula-ursi auricula
intro: Auricula-ursi auricula, also known as bearberry or kinnikinnick, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to Northern Europe and Asia, characterized by its small, white or pink flowers and red or yellow berries. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and treat urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and other genitourinary issues. The health benefits of auricula-ursi include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting overall urinary health. Herbal preparations of auricula-ursi can be found in teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are often used to treat urinary tract infections and other genitourinary conditions.
